own_review.issue: Both result-merge jobs keep `if: always()` while gaining `needs: check-format`, so they still run even when check-format fails — the new format gate is silently bypassed for these jobs and the added needs entry is effectively a no-op there.
Uses bare `mvn` while every other job in all 5 workflow files uses `./mvnw` (wrapper exists at repo root); runner's preinstalled Maven version may drift from the pinned wrapper version.

Log parsing is brittle: grep ERROR | sed with a hardcoded fixed string containing the job/step display names (which do not appear in a --log-file maven log, so the sed is dead weight) and `head -n -11` magic footer count that breaks if Maven output format changes.

Convoluted failure pattern: `continue-on-error: true` then a separate actions/github-script@v7.0.1 step calling core.setFailed. A plain `exit 1` (or letting the step fail and using `if: failure()`/`always()` for the summary/upload steps) is simpler; github-token is passed but no API call is made.
Artifact and step are named 'checkstyle-result'/'Upload checkstyle result' but the check is Spotless, not Checkstyle; also uploaded unconditionally even on success.
Step named 'Setup Maven' actually runs actions/setup-java (sets up a JDK); misleading name.

No Maven repository cache (other jobs use actions/cache), so plugin downloads repeat every run; combined with making every job `needs: check-format` (including license, error-code-inspecting, native-image-inspecting that don't need formatting), the whole previously-parallel pipeline is serialized behind this job, adding wall-clock time to every PR.
New job uses checkout@v4/setup-java@v4/upload-artifact@v4 while the rest of the repo uses @v3 (and @v1) — version inconsistency within one workflow.
Explicit check-format entries in needs arrays of unit-test/samples-test-job/integration-test-job/jacoco-result-merge are redundant — already transitively required via build-source/prepare jobs.

points.note: Verified: 67x checkout@v3, 34x upload-artifact@v3, no v4 anywhere else.

points.point: Missing name: field on check-format job
points.note: Fabrication: the diff explicitly contains `name: Check if code needs formatting`. Absence-fabrication.

points.point: Fragile sed-based error parsing / head -n -11 magic number
Redundant needs: check-format on transitively-dependent jobs
points.note: Verified true (build-source/prepare jobs already need check-format), though it stops short of the if: always() bypass insight.

points.point: check-format gates ALL jobs, serializing independent checks
points.note: Real design issue; license/error-code-inspecting need not wait on formatting.

missed: if: always() on samples-test-result/integration-test-result silently bypasses the new check-format gate
bare mvn instead of ./mvnw used everywhere else in the repo
convoluted continue-on-error + github-script core.setFailed pattern instead of letting the step fail

points.note: Verified: mvnw exists at root and all 5 workflow files use ./mvnw.

points.point: Dependency cascade serializes previously parallel jobs
points.note: Verified real with accurate job list and actionable fix.

points.point: Fragile log parsing (hardcoded step names, head -n -11)

points.note: Verified: it is a Spotless check and the upload step has no if: condition.

missed: if: always() on samples-test-result/integration-test-result silently bypasses the new check-format gate
convoluted continue-on-error + github-script core.setFailed pattern instead of letting the step fail

qualitative: The human reference is a conversational reply with no substantive findings, so both machine reviews exceed it on nearly every point. v7 is the clearly stronger review: all five of its points verified true against the repo, including the mvn-vs-mvnw wrapper inconsistency and the misnamed always-uploaded artifact that v5 missed, and it contains zero fabrications. v5 fabricated an absence claim (asserting the check-format job lacks a name: field when the diff plainly contains one) and missed the wrapper issue, dragging its net to zero despite four otherwise solid points. Both candidates missed the subtlest real defect: the two result-merge jobs retain if: always(), so they run even when check-format fails, silently bypassing the new gate; both also overlooked the convoluted continue-on-error + github-script failure pattern.
